idea.title: Reverse Tycoon
idea.hook: Inherit a thriving cafe and DEMOLISH it tile by tile to satisfy creditors — but each demolition kills customers whose tips were funding the next debt payment.
idea.hook_ru: Унаследуйте процветающее кафе и сносите его по плитке, чтобы расплатиться с кредиторами — каждый снос убивает доход, который должен платить следующий долг.
idea.prototype: 8x8 cafe grid pre-filled with tables (cost 40 / income 2), grills (cost 80 / income 3), espresso machines (cost 120 / income 5). Player owes 1500 coins by turn 15. Each turn: demolish ONE tile (refund = 60% of build cost added to debt-payment). After demolish, end-of-turn income from remaining alive stations also pays the debt. Demolishing kills that station's per-turn income permanently. Adjacency synergies (+1/turn between adjacent stations of DIFFERENT types) break when neighbors die. Win = debt <= 0 within 15 turns. Lose = turn 15 ends with debt > 0. Non-timer pressure: 15-turn moves budget + 1500-coin debt target + resource scarcity / synergy-cascade penalty.
idea.mutation_of: Poki 'Monkey Mart' / 'My Perfect Hotel' / 'Cat Snack Bar' tycoon dominance inverted. Crazygames 'Bills Must Be Paid' provides direct thematic signal.
idea.why_novel: Every tycoon on Poki/Crazygames adds upward; zero ship games where the loop is destruction-for-cash with synergy collapse penalties. The optimization is REVERSE-knapsack of which producers die first.
idea.non_timer_pressure: 15-turn moves budget + 1500-coin debt score-target + resource scarcity (income drops permanently per demolish) — three stacked, no countdown timer anywhere
idea.lose_condition: turn > MAX_TURNS (15) with debt still positive — gs becomes GAMEOVER

sources.title: Bills Must Be Paid
sources.snippet: Direct thematic signal — debt-pressure casual game on CrazyGames demonstrating audience appetite for paycheck-pressure framing.

sources.title: Monkey Mart
sources.snippet: Top of Poki's tycoon-dominance evidence pile — proves grid-based station-management is a portable mass-audience loop.

sources.title: My Perfect Hotel
sources.snippet: Tile-by-tile build-up tycoon — the polar opposite of the loop Reverse Tycoon inverts.

sources.title: Cat Snack Bar
sources.snippet: Cafe-themed station-management tycoon — confirms cafe is a workable visual frame and the same crowd will recognise the inversion.

synthesizer_brief: Telegram-digest synthesis flagged Poki's tycoon dominance (Monkey Mart, My Perfect Hotel, Cat Snack Bar) and CrazyGames' Bills Must Be Paid as a thematic cluster nobody has inverted. The novel loop is reverse-knapsack: every demolish gives a refund AND breaks the income engine, so the optimal sell-order is non-trivial. The 15-turn budget + 1500-coin debt with declining income produces a tight ~30-second decision per turn, single-session shape that fits the gallery's casual-strategy slot.

alternatives.title: Echo Cartographer
alternatives.hook: Ping unseen caves with sonar pulses and draw the map yourself by clicking where you THINK the walls are.
alternatives.mutation_of: Echo Locator (shipped) inverted + Pixolve daily nonogram signal
alternatives.why_novel: Player commits predictions before knowing — no shipped game makes you the cartographer with punishment for wrong marks.

alternatives.title: Mirror Match
alternatives.hook: Two armies on opposite sides of the board move in perfect mirror to your input — find the one move that lets your knight escape while the enemy mirror crashes into its own wall.
alternatives.mutation_of: r/WebGames 'Spark Relay' + Ultimate Tic-Tac-Toe coupled-move pattern
alternatives.why_novel: Coupled-mirror-input puzzle with asymmetric walls — same input has different outcomes per side.

alternatives.title: Wishlist Saboteur
alternatives.hook: You're a competitor's spy — drag your rival's Steam capsule features into the WRONG genre bucket to tank their algorithm placement.
alternatives.mutation_of: Demo Trimmer reverse-knapsack + Tim's Wishlist Climb (inverted polarity)
alternatives.why_novel: Sabotage-tycoon-meta-gamedev hybrid with bluff tension from periodic bucket inspections.

alternatives.title: Anti-Match
alternatives.hook: Inverted match-3 — break up clusters instead of forming them.
alternatives.why_novel: Cluster-disruption verb absent from puzzle portals.
